justification: All three ACs present with exact values ($47.80→47, $0.99→0, 12-month expiry). Both domain rules present (1pt/dollar floor-rounded, 12-month inactivity expiry). Both architecture functions referenced (earnPoints, checkExpiry) with implementation notes (Math.floor, date comparison). 365-day vs 12-month terminology explicitly flagged in open_questions with leap-year discussion. No invented information — open questions correctly identify gaps rather than fabricating answers.

observations.text: AC2 ($0.99→0) explicitly preserved as a separate acceptance criterion testing the floor edge case
365-day vs 12-month discrepancy flagged thoroughly in open_questions with concrete resolution guidance (calendar-month subtraction vs explicit simplification)
Correctly identifies Bird as domain authority for resolving the 12-month vs 365-day conflict
No invented features — does not add redemption, tiers, or other loyalty program concepts not in source outputs
loyalty.ts referenced as target file in task_context
Additional open questions (return types, persistence) are genuine gaps flagged without fabricating answers
Preserves exact wording 'no purchase for 12 calendar months' rather than vague 'inactive accounts'

terminology_alignment section does not directly flag the 12-months vs 365-days discrepancy — that insight is in open_questions instead, which still satisfies the rubric but splits the information across sections
